text: This is an intraoperative image following instrumentation.
A temporary rod was placed, cranials to the left and medials to the top of the screen.
The tumor and spinal cord are labeled here with white arrows.
This is an intraoperative video showing the osteotomy cut at the medial pedicle at the level of T9.
An osteotome and mallet were used initially, followed by a paddle shiver.
As the interior aspect of the vertebral body was approached, the osmotic cut was visualized using fluoroscopy.
This is a interoperative picture showing the final construct following the stage one instrumentation and osteotomy.
Of note, side connectors were used to connect the titanium rods to the primary cobalt chrome rod.
The patient was positioned in the left lateral gibbous position with Allen arm used for support with axillary roll.
The planned right thoracotomy incision can be seen here with the tumor noted in dotted lines.
Following disconnection of the tumor from the anterior longitudinal ligament, the tumor was removed and blocked with the help of cardiothoracic surgery.
Of note, respect was paid to the diaphragm, esophagus, as well as the heart during this removal.
This is the intraoperative picture showing the pericardium and diaphragm
The lateral plate can be visualized at the bottom of the screen.
The tumor was sent for pathology.
The tumor measured approximately 15 centimeters in length.
